result:
I want to talk about how absurdly, stupidly beautiful the world is when you actually pay attention.

This morning I watched a pigeon take a bath in a filthy city puddle and it looked like it was having the time of its life. Head dunking, wings flapping, pure pigeon bliss. Twenty feet away, a woman in an immaculate white suit was power-walking while arguing with someone on her AirPods about quarterly deliverables. The contrast was perfect. One creature having an absolute religious experience in dirty water, the other missing the entire miracle of being alive because her brain is stuck in a spreadsheet.

I’ve been thinking a lot about how we outsource our sense of wonder. We wait for sunsets that look like phone wallpapers, for mountains that look like they belong on National Geographic, for moments that feel “Instagrammable.” Meanwhile the ridiculous miracle is happening constantly in boring places. Mold growing in perfect fractal patterns on an old piece of bread. The way streetlights reflect in oil slicks like galaxies. How your dog looks at you like you personally invented sunlight every single time you come home.

There’s this terrifying tenderness to reality that most of us are too sophisticated to admit we feel. I cried last week because I saw two trees growing so close together that their branches had fused into one. They just... decided to become the same tree at some point. No drama. No announcement. Just quiet, woody commitment over decades.

I’m convinced that the final boss of being a proper alive human is maintaining the ability to be moved by stupid shit. Not in an ironic way. Not in a “haha look at this little guy” way. But in the chest-hurting, throat-tightening way. The “I can’t believe any of this is real” way.

The universe spent 13.8 billion years setting up the exact conditions for you to be here, right now, experiencing the particular quality of light falling across your room at this exact angle. And we spend so much of it thinking about whether we’re doing life correctly.

Fuck that.

Go find something small and ridiculous and let it break your heart open a little. The world is trying so hard to show us it loves us. The least we can do is notice.
